title: This Is Water
author: David Foster Wallace
description: Kenyon College commencement address on empathy, consciousness, and the difficulty of living deliberately. A short, profound meditation on adult life and mindful awareness.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
description: Explores two systems of thought — System 1 (fast, intuitive) vs System 2 (slow, deliberate) — synthesizing decades of research on cognitive biases, heuristics, and decision-making.

title: How the Mind Works
author: Steven Pinker
description: Reverse-engineers human cognition through evolutionary psychology — explaining perception, emotion, language, art, and consciousness. A landmark work in cognitive science.

title: The Alignment Problem
author: Brian Christian
description: An in-depth exploration of building AI systems aligned with human values, based on interviews with leading researchers. Covers prediction, reinforcement learning, and value alignment.

title: On Intelligence
author: Jeff Hawkins
description: Proposes the Memory-Prediction Framework (HTM): the brain as a predictive hierarchy. Hawkins (Palm/Numenta founder) argues intelligence emerges from hierarchical temporal memory.

title: A Brief History of Intelligence
author: Max S. Bennett
description: Traces 5 key evolutionary breakthroughs in brain development from bilaterians to humans and explores their implications for building general AI. Academic companion: 'Five breakthroughs' (Frontiers 2021).
